title: Fuzz Door Slam
prompt: Create an original garage rock track at 138 BPM in E minor, with raw two-guitar attack, loose push-pull energy, and a sweaty basement pulse. Use cracking snare, trashy ride, forward kick, short human fills; bass stays gritty and repetitive with rough passing tones. Guitars: overdriven barre chords, ragged hooks, noisy downstrokes, plus amp hum, handclaps, and feedback swells. Vocal texture: urgent, nasal, half-shouted, close to the mic. Structure: noise-and-riff intro, lean verse with bass and dry drums, pre-chorus with harmony guitar and snare lift, full chorus with wider chords, eight-bar bridge or solo break, then final chorus into tight outro. Production: dry, raw, mono-leaning, unvarnished; mix/master: sharp guitars, strong snare crack, moderate loudness, no glossy pop finish.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, EDM drop, trap hi-hats, over-polished pop sheen, fake crowd chants, brittle cymbals, muddy guitars, weak snare, generic radio pop
